instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap casts to Character. If cast succeeds, print Player. If cast fails, print Not A Player.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SafeCast
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Not A Player"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n4.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SafeCastExample
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Not A Player"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n4.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SafeCastExample
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Not A Player"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n4.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap casts the other actor to Pawn. If cast succeeds get the pawn's controller and print its name. If cast fails print Unknown.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastToPawn
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToPawn
NODE n3: GetController
NODE n4: GetDisplayName
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Unknown"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.AsPawn -> n3.Target [Pawn]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Target [Controller]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n6.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PawnInfo
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToPawn
NODE n3: GetController
NODE n4: PrintString
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Unknown"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Target [Actor]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.InString [String]
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n5.ExecuteЎыџNЎыџN
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PawnInfo
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToPawn
NODE n3: GetController
NODE n4: PrintString
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Unknown"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Target [Actor]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.InString [String]
EXEC n2.CastFailed -> n5.ExecuteЎыџNЎыџN

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a float variable Speed. On overlap cast to Character. If success, get character movement component and set max walk speed to Speed variable.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SetSpeed
PARENT: Actor
VAR Speed: Float = 600.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: GetCharacterMovement
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName=Speed]
NODE n5: SetMaxWalkSpeed
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Target [Character]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.Target [CharacterMovementComponent]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.MaxWalkSpeed [Float]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SetSpeed
PARENT: Actor
VAR Speed: Float = 600.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: GetCharacterMovement
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName=Speed]
NODE n5: SetMaxWalkSpeed
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Target [Character]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.Target [CharacterMovementComponent]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.MaxWalkSpeed [Float]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SetSpeed
PARENT: Actor
VAR Speed: Float = 600.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: GetCharacterMovement
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName=Speed]
NODE n5: SetMaxWalkSpeed
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.AsCharacter -> n3.Target [Character]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.Target [CharacterMovementComponent]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.MaxWalkSpeed [Float]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay uses a Sequence with 3 outputs. Output A prints Hello, output B prints World, output C prints Done.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SeqFanOut
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Hello"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="World"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Done"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.C -> n5.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TripleSequence
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Hello"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="World"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Done"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.C -> n5.Executeıldığında
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TripleSequence
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Hello"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="World"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Done"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.C -> n5.Executeıldığında

instruction: Create a Blueprint with float variables X, Y, Z. On BeginPlay use a Sequence with 4 outputs. A prints X, B prints Y, C prints Z, D prints the sum of all three.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PrintCoords
PARENT: Actor
VAR X: Float = 1.0
VAR Y: Float = 2.0
VAR Z: Float = 3.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: VariableGet [VarName=X]
NODE n4: PrintString
NODE n5: VariableGet [VarName=Y]
NODE n6: PrintString
NODE n7: VariableGet [VarName=Z]
NODE n8: PrintString
NODE n9: AddFloat
NODE n10: AddFloat
NODE n11: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n4.Execute
DATA n3.Value -> n4.InString [String]
EXEC n2.B -> n6.Execute
DATA n5.Value -> n6.InString [String]
EXEC n2.C -> n8.Execute
DATA n7.Value -> n8.InString [String]
EXEC n2.D -> n11.Execute
DATA n3.Value -> n9.A [Float]
DATA n5.Value -> n9.B [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.A [Float]
DATA n7.Value -> n10.B [Float]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.InString [String]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PrintCoords
PARENT: Actor
VAR X: Float = 0.0
VAR Y: Float = 0.0
VAR Z: Float = 0.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=X]
NODE n3: PrintString
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName=Y]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: VariableGet [VarName=Z]
NODE n7: PrintString
NODE n8: AddFloat
NODE n9: AddFloat
NODE n10: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.InString [String]
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.InString [String]
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n7.InString [String]
EXEC n7.Then -> n10.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n8.A [Float]
DATA n4.Value -> n8.B [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.A [Float]
DATA n6.Value -> n9.B [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.InString [String]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PrintCoords
PARENT: Actor
VAR X: Float = 0.0
VAR Y: Float = 0.0
VAR Z: Float = 0.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=X]
NODE n3: PrintString
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName=Y]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: VariableGet [VarName=Z]
NODE n7: PrintString
NODE n8: AddFloat
NODE n9: AddFloat
NODE n10: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.InString [String]
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.InString [String]
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n7.InString [String]
EXEC n7.Then -> n10.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n8.A [Float]
DATA n4.Value -> n8.B [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.A [Float]
DATA n6.Value -> n9.B [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay uses a Sequence with 5 outputs. Each output prints a different day: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Weekdays
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Monday"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Tuesday"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Wednesday"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Thursday"]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Friday"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.C -> n5.Execute
EXEC n2.D -> n6.Execute
EXEC n2.E -> n7.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Weekdays
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Monday"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Tuesday"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Wednesday"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Thursday"]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Friday"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_0 -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_1 -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_2 -> n5.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_3 -> n6.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_4 -> n7.Execute
raw_output:

PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Monday"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Tuesday"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Wednesday"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Thursday"]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Friday"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_0 -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_1 -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_2 -> n5.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_3 -> n6.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_4 -> n7.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with float Health at 100. On AnyDamage subtract the damage, check if health is below zero, and if so print Dead and destroy the actor.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageCheck
PARENT: Actor
VAR Health: Float = 100.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: VariableSet [VarName=Health]
NODE n5: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n8: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TakeDamage
PARENT: Actor
VAR Health: Float = 100.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: VariableSet [VarName=Health]
NODE n5: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n8: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TakeDamage
PARENT: Actor
VAR Health: Float = 100.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: VariableSet [VarName=Health]
NODE n5: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n8: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
